comments.author: chatgpt-codex-connector
comments.body:
Codex usage limits have been reached for code reviews. Please check with the admins of this repo to increase the limits by adding credits.
Credits must be used to enable repository wide code reviews.

reviews.author: linyueqian
reviews.body:
Went through the streaming implementation end to end (codec stream session, raw async-chunk processor, talker MTP refactor, serving changes). The core design looks solid: the per-request isolation guards from #4415 are preserved, the raw-path threshold/flush logic checks out, the widened chunk-adapter merge does not affect other models' list-mode codes, and error propagation in serving_speech is unchanged. Left three inline comments (one High on slot lifecycle, two Medium).

A few smaller things, not blocking:

- `benchmarks/patch/patch.py`: the Seed-TTS WER fallback assigns raw `bytes(pcm_capture)` when normalization fails; for this model that can be 48 kHz stereo flowing into an eval path that assumes 24 kHz mono, silently skewing WER. Failing/skipping the sample would be safer than falling back.
- `configuration_moss_audio_tokenizer_v2.py` docstring defaults (24000 / 1920 / rvq) contradict the actual signature defaults (48000 / 3840 / rlfq), and the `MossTTSCodecDecoder` class docstring (24 kHz mono, n_vq 32/16, single shared checkpoint) is now stale for the v2 path. Same for the `forward()` example in `audio_tokenizer_v2.py` which builds a mono 24 kHz input.
- The two new vendored files could carry the same "Vendored from ..." provenance header that the sibling `audio_tokenizer.py` uses (upstream repo, revision, what was changed). ~2300 lines of vendored code without it will be hard to sync later.

Also, since this is the first time stage 1 runs concurrent for this family

reviews.author: hsliuustc0106
reviews.body: Left two inline comments from my review.

reviews.author: hsliuustc0106
reviews.body: Reviewing the streaming + seed + codec changes. A few non-blocking notes below — overall the refactor (splitting delay vs. raw async-chunk paths, threading the generator through `talker_mtp`, and the vendored tokenizer-v2) looks sound.
